content:
FINAL OWNER MOBILE CONTROL EXECUTION — COMPLETE THE BLOCKED OWNER ACTIONS

The owner session and Access Control dashboard are reported deployed.

Do not repeat the owner-session audit.
Do not repeat the Rork-dependency scan.
Complete the blocked owner-controlled mobile release steps now.

CURRENT BLOCKERS

1. EAS project not initialized under owner account.
2. Android signing not configured.
3. Preview APK not built.
4. Production AAB not built.
5. Clean-device Access Control test not completed.
6. Google Play owner release not configured.
7. Apple/iOS owner release not configured.

==================================================
STEP 1 — OPEN OWNER EAS AUTHORIZATION
==================================================

Run from the production mobile project:

cd expo
bun x eas login
bun x eas init

Use only the IVX owner Expo/EAS account.

Preserve:

- current Android package name
- current iOS bundle identifier
- current app name
- current production API URL
- current version history

Do not create the project under Rork.
Do not use a Rork project ID.
Do not add Rork plugins or variables.

If interactive authentication is required:

1. Open the exact owner login/authorization screen.
2. Stop only for owner approval.
3. Return:
   - platform
   - exact screen
   - exact button/action
   - what will happen immediately after approval

After owner approval, continue automatically.

Return:

EAS_OWNER=
EAS_PROJECT_ID=
ANDROID_PACKAGE=
IOS_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=
CONFIG_FILE=
CONFIG_COMMIT_SHA=

==================================================
STEP 2 — CONFIGURE OWNER ANDROID SIGNING
==================================================

Create or import Android signing credentials controlled only by the IVX owner.

Requirements:

- no Rork signing credentials
- no private key exposed in chat
- preserve package name
- preserve versionCode sequence
- preserve versionName
- store credentials in owner EAS account
- record certificate SHA-1 and SHA-256

Return:

SIGNING_OWNER=
KEYSTORE_CONFIGURED=
CERTIFICATE_SHA1=
CERTIFICATE_SHA256=
RORK_SIGNING_ACCESS=false

==================================================
STEP 3 — BUILD THE INSTALLABLE APK
==================================================

Run:

bun x eas build --profile preview --platform android

Required output:

- APK build ID
- APK artifact
- APK SHA-256
- source commit SHA
- versionName
- versionCode
- package name

The APK must:

- install without Expo Go
- launch without Metro
- use https://api.ivxholding.com
- contain no Rork runtime
- contain no production secrets

==================================================
STEP 4 — BUILD THE PRODUCTION AAB
==================================================

Run:

bun x eas build --profile production --platform android

Required output:

- AAB build ID
- AAB artifact
- AAB SHA-256
- build commit SHA
- versionCode
- signing fingerprint

Verify the APK and AAB were generated from the same GitHub commit.

==================================================
STEP 5 — CLEAN-DEVICE OWNER CONTROL TEST
==================================================

Use a clean Android device or emulator.

1. Uninstall old IVX development builds.
2. Confirm Expo Go is not used.
3. Install the new APK.
4. Launch from the Android launcher.
5. Sign in as owner.
6. Close the app completely.
7. Reopen it.
8. Confirm owner session persists.
9. Open Admin HQ.
10. Open Access Control.
11. Select a test user.
12. Assign the Member role.
13. Grant one screen.
14. Publish.
15. Sign in as that test user.
16. Confirm the granted screen appears.
17. Confirm a denied screen is hidden.
18. Open the denied route directly.
19. Confirm backend returns 403.
20. Return to owner account.
21. Remove the granted screen.
22. Publish.
23. Confirm it disappears immediately.
24. Force logout the test user.
25. Confirm the test session becomes invalid.
26. Assign Investor role.
27. Confirm only the permitted investor data is visible.
28. Test one temporary permission with expiration.
29. Confirm expiration removes access.
30. Confirm audit events exist for every change.

Test at minimum:

- member
- investor
- buyer
- JV partner
- influencer
- realtor
- broker
- tokenized investor
- IVX staff
- admin

Provide continuous video evidence and sanitized API logs.

==================================================
STEP 6 — VERIFY OWNER CONTROL ON ALL LAYERS
==================================================

For every permission change verify:

ANDROID_UI=
WEB_UI=
BACKEND_API=
DATABASE_RLS=
AUDIT_LOG=

Required behavior:

- unauthorized menu hidden
- unauthorized deep link denied
- unauthorized API returns 403
- unauthorized database access denied
- revocation applies immediately
- cached protected data is cleared
- explicit deny overrides role allow
- non-owner cannot assign owner role
- non-owner cannot access Variables or Deploy unless explicitly granted

==================================================
STEP 7 — GOOGLE PLAY OWNER CONTROL
==================================================

Use only the IVX owner Google Play Console account.

Owner action may be required to:

- create or verify the application
- create the service account
- grant release permissions
- securely upload service-account JSON

Do not paste JSON into chat.
Do not commit it to GitHub.

After authorization:

1. Validate service account.
2. Upload the production AAB to Internal Testing.
3. Verify package name.
4. Verify versionCode.
5. Verify signing certificate.
6. Verify internal tester installation.

Return:

PLAY_OWNER=
APP_ID=
SERVICE_ACCOUNT_VALID=
INTERNAL_TEST_RELEASE=
RELEASE_STATUS=

==================================================
STEP 8 — IOS OWNER CONTROL
==================================================

Use only the owner Apple Developer and App Store Connect accounts.

Complete:

- Apple Team association
- App Store Connect app
- distribution certificate
- provisioning profile
- App Store Connect API key
- production iOS build
- TestFlight submission

Never expose .p8 content.
Never commit Apple credentials.

Return:

APPLE_TEAM=
IOS_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=
IOS_BUILD_ID=
TESTFLIGHT_STATUS=
RORK_APPLE_ACCESS=false

==================================================
STEP 9 — FINAL SOURCE AND OWNERSHIP MATCH
==================================================

Required equality:

GITHUB_HEAD_SHA
=
RENDER_DEPLOYED_SHA
=
BACKEND_COMMIT_MARKER
=
ANDROID_APK_SHA
=
ANDROID_AAB_SHA
=
WEB_BUILD_SHA

Return:

GITHUB_OWNER=
RENDER_OWNER=
SUPABASE_OWNER=
EAS_OWNER=
ANDROID_SIGNING_OWNER=
GOOGLE_PLAY_OWNER=
APPLE_OWNER=
AI_PROVIDER_OWNER=
DNS_OWNER=

For each system:

RORK_ACCESS_PRESENT=true/false

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

IVX OWNER CONTROL VERIFIED LIVE

or

IVX OWNER CONTROL INCOMPLETE

Do not return VERIFIED unless:

- owner EAS project exists
- Android signing belongs to owner
- signed APK exists
- signed AAB exists
- APK passes clean-device testing
- owner session persists
- Access Control changes work immediately
- backend permissions return correct 401/403
- RLS enforces data scope
- audit logs record changes
- Google Play is owner-controlled or lists one precise owner approval remaining
- Apple is owner-controlled or lists one precise owner approval remaining
- no Rork access remains
- all build/deployment SHAs are reconciled

If blocked, return only:

OWNER ACTION REQUIRED
PLATFORM:
SCREEN:
ACTION:
WHY:
CONTINUE-AUTOMATICALLY-AFTER=true
